question: What is the primary purpose of the scope resolution operator (::) in C++? Provide a simple example of its use.
expectedPoints: Used to access a global variable when a local variable of the same name exists.
Used to define a member function outside of its class declaration.
Used to access static members of a class.

question: Explain how the scope resolution operator is used to resolve the 'namespace' ambiguity issue in C++. Provide a code example to illustrate your answer.
expectedPoints: It allows access to variables/functions within a specific namespace, preventing conflicts.
It is crucial for using standard library components (e.g., `std::cout`).

question: Describe in detail three distinct and important use cases for the scope resolution operator in C++ Object-Oriented Programming. For each use case, provide a brief code snippet and explain the problem it solves.
expectedPoints: Defining member functions outside the class body to separate interface from implementation.
Accessing static class members, which are associated with the class itself rather than individual objects.
Resolving multiple inheritance ambiguities when a derived class inherits from two base classes with identical member names.
Accessing overridden base class functions from a derived class.
